artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one policy-specific Hokkaido University Open Education Center page, written in Japanese with an English section, giving notes to faculty and students on generative AI use in education and learning. Official HUSCAP repository and higher-education training center pages were rejected as repository/event material rather than policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stage only official Hokkaido University sources.
Do not use repository records, event pages, search snippets, or course references as policy evidence.
Do not stage raw HTML, PDFs, screenshots, or browser profiles.
Scope claims to the 2023 notes on generative AI use in education and learning.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 生成系AIの利用に関する留意事項 Notes on the Use of Generative AI

artifacts.claimText: Hokkaido University's 2023 notes encourage faculty and students to explore generative AI methods that can enhance education and learning, while using the technology cautiously in light of educational concerns.

citation.citationTitle: Hokkaido University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For faculty use, Hokkaido University's notes say instructors should explain in advance whether generative AI is permitted in class and how it is restricted, and should consider grading fairness when students use it in assignments or practical learning.

citation.citationTitle: Hokkaido University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For student use, Hokkaido University's notes say students preparing reports and other assignments should follow the instructor's guidance and instructions regarding generative AI use.

citation.citationTitle: Hokkaido University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: When generative AI is used to assist with grading submitted assignments, Hokkaido University's notes warn faculty not to enter students' personal or private information because entered data may be stored and output to other users.

citation.citationTitle: Hokkaido University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Hokkaido University's notes warn students that ChatGPT-like outputs may unknowingly plagiarize internet text, may contain inaccuracies, and may have unclear or inaccurate sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 生成系AIの利用に関する留意事項 Notes on the Use of Generative AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 本学の教員及び学生のみなさまには、生成系AIの利用に際し、本学の教育理念に照らしながら、このような技術を用いて教育学習を高度化する手法を探究すると同時に、教育利用についての懸念を踏まえ、慎重な利用を心がけてください。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Faculty and students are asked to explore methods for enhancing education and learning with generative AI while using it cautiously in light of educational concerns.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about the 2023 Hokkaido University notes for generative AI use in education and learning.
Approved because the claim follows the faculty-use item without expanding it beyond class permission, restriction, and grading fairness guidance.
Approved as a direct student-assignment guidance claim tied to the instructor-instructions sentence.
Approved because the source directly warns against entering students' personal or private information when using generative AI to assist grading.
Approved as a narrow risk statement based on the student-use cautions about plagiarism, inaccuracies, and unclear sources.

artifacts.title: Hokkaido University generative AI guidance staged review
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source-only claims from Hokkaido University's 2023 notes on generative AI use. The evidence supports cautious educational use, faculty disclosure of class rules, instructor-specific student assignment guidance, privacy limits for grading assistance, and student cautions about plagiarism and inaccurate AI output.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to Hokkaido University's 2023 notes on generative AI use in education and learning; the source states that the university planned continued review and separate future guidelines.
Rejected official event and repository pages were not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
